title: Why Supabase?
intro: We're using Supabase as our backend for the task manager. Swipe right on true Supabase advantages, left on misconceptions or things that aren't quite right.

teach.title: Supabase = Instant Backend
teach.body: Supabase gives you a PostgreSQL database, authentication, file storage, and real-time subscriptions — all from one dashboard. Instead of stitching together five services, you get one platform that handles the backend so you can focus on the frontend.

teach.title: Supabase is a Furnished Apartment
teach.body: Building a backend from scratch is like moving into an empty house — you need to buy every piece of furniture. Supabase is a furnished apartment. The database, auth, and storage are already set up. You can rearrange things, but you're not starting from zero.
Supabase provides PostgreSQL, auth, storage, and real-time features with a generous free tier. For a task manager, it covers every backend need without requiring you to build or manage a server.

cards.content: Supabase includes built-in authentication, so you don't need to build login/signup from scratch
cards.explanation: Auth is one of the hardest things to build securely. Supabase handles email/password, OAuth, magic links, and more — so your task manager gets secure login for free.

cards.content: You never need to write or understand SQL when using Supabase
cards.explanation: Supabase has a JavaScript client that abstracts many queries, but understanding SQL is still important. You'll need it for Row Level Security policies, complex queries, and debugging.

cards.content: Supabase has a free tier that's more than enough for building and launching a personal project like our task manager
cards.explanation: The free tier includes 500 MB of database storage, 50,000 monthly active users for auth, and 1 GB of file storage. Our task manager won't come close to those limits.

cards.content: Supabase replaces every backend tool you'll ever need — you'll never have to use anything else
cards.explanation: Supabase is great for data, auth, and storage, but it doesn't handle things like email sending, payment processing, or complex background jobs. You'll still need other services for those.

cards.content: Supabase uses Row Level Security so users can only access their own data — a critical feature for our multi-user task manager
cards.explanation: RLS policies are enforced at the database level. Even if someone bypasses your frontend, they can't read other users' tasks. This is security done right.

cards.content: Using Supabase means you're permanently locked in and can never switch to another provider
cards.explanation: Supabase is open source and runs on standard PostgreSQL. Your data, schema, and even RLS policies are portable. You can export and host it yourself anytime.

cards.content: Supabase offers real-time subscriptions, so your task list can update instantly when data changes
cards.explanation: Real-time is built in. If you later add collaboration features, tasks update live without refreshing. Even for a single user, it keeps the UI in sync.
